page: Microsoft Azure EC128es_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ECEV5 server family. It features 128 dedicated virtual CPUs powered by an Intel processor and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 6.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external network-attached storage options, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are no onboard GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning workloads. Additionally, the instance includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC128es_v5 offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its 128 dedicated vCPUs, positioning it as a specialized resource for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is designed for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning applications, and large-scale data analytics where massive memory capacity and high CPU core counts are required to process large datasets concurrently.
description: Microsoft Azure EC128es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability for network-attached storage solutions. It contains no graphics processing units and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration is engineered for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated compute resources make it highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics pipelines that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or hardware acceleration.
